quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 6 Quiz 10
quiz.subtitle: Refraction Applications — Depth, TIR, Lenses, and Prisms
quiz.subject: Physics
quiz.topic: Grade 10 - Waves and Simple Harmonic Motion

questions.question: A swimming pool has a real depth of $2.0\text{ m}$. The refractive index of water is $1.33$. What is the apparent depth of the pool when viewed from directly above?
A fish appears to be at a depth of $0.60\text{ m}$ below the water surface. If the refractive index of water is $1.33$, what is its real depth?
The refractive index of glass is $1.5$. What is the critical angle for light traveling from glass into air? ($\sin^{-1}(0.667) = 41.8°$)
Light travels from diamond ($n = 2.42$) into air. What is the critical angle? ($\sin^{-1}(0.413) = 24.4°$)
A converging (convex) lens is:
options: Thicker at the center than at the edges
Thinner at the center than at the edges
questions.explanation: A converging lens bulges outward at the center, making it thicker in the middle than at the edges.

questions.question: When parallel rays of light pass through a converging lens, they:
options: Converge to a point (the focal point)
questions.explanation: A converging lens brings parallel rays together at the focal point.

questions.question: A diverging (concave) lens causes parallel light rays to:
options: Spread out as if coming from a focal point
questions.explanation: A diverging lens spreads parallel rays outward so they appear to originate from a virtual focal point.

questions.question: When white light passes through a triangular glass prism and splits into a spectrum, this effect is called:
questions.explanation: Dispersion is the splitting of white light into its constituent colors due to different wavelengths refracting by different amounts.

questions.question: When white light is dispersed by a prism, which color is deviated (bent) the most?
questions.explanation: Violet light has the shortest wavelength and is refracted the most. Red light has the longest wavelength and is refracted the least.

questions.question: Dispersion occurs in a prism because:
options: Different colors travel at the same speed in glass
Different colors travel at different speeds in glass
The prism reflects different colors at different angles
questions.explanation: Each color has a slightly different speed in glass, causing each to refract at a slightly different angle — this is dispersion.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered real/apparent depth, critical angle, lenses, and prisms!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the critical angle formula $\sin c = 1/n$ and the behavior of converging vs. diverging lenses.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on $n = d_{real}/d_{apparent}$ and which colors bend most in a prism.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: real vs. apparent depth, total internal reflection, lens types, and dispersion.
